channel.title: NMP Status Report
channel.description: Automated engineering status audio for the nmp (Nostr Mobile Primitives) project -- an embeddable Nostr client engine (Rust core + Swift/Kotlin SDKs). Each episode is a self-contained, candid, technical briefing for the human operator.
channel.itunes:author: nmp status reporter
channel.itunes:summary: Automated, self-contained engineering status audio for the nmp project: what it is, what it can do today, and how solid it is.

item.title: NMP Briefing — 2026-07-15 11:30 UTC
item.description:
WHAT NMP IS -- NMP (Nostr Mobile Primitives) is an embeddable Nostr client engine: a Rust core with Swift and Kotlin SDKs, owning the machinery apps otherwise rebuild badly (event store, relay connections, subscription routing, canonical state, signing, durable publishing). Two nouns: a live query (a declarative demand observed through your platform's native reactive primitive) and a write intent (a durable publish obligation tracked through a reattachable receipt). A library you call, not a framework you live inside.
WHAT IT CAN DO TODAY. Reading & state: declarative live queries with reactive bindings; a redb-backed canonical store with provenance-preserving dedup, replaceable events, NIP-40 expiry, kind:5 deletion, and negative-delta supersession; snapshots return rows plus scoped evidence, never a global "synced". Relays: full connection lifecycle with one finite fan-out ceiling; self-bootstrapping NIP-65 outbox routing (configure only indexers); parse-once ingest with bounded parallel verification; NIP-11 metadata and NIP-77 negentropy wired; NIP-42 AUTH partial. Signing: local keys plus full NIP-46 bunker with reconnect and a bounded sign-only path across all four surfaces; Keychain/Keystore providers and secret zeroization are gaps. Publishing: durable write intents with atomic Accepted, pending rows, signature promotion, compensation, and a bounded-retry outbox; at-most-once ambiguity becomes OutcomeUnknown. Modules (opt-in): NIP-02 following (Swift + Kotlin), NIP-29 groups, NIP-51 relay-list reading with editing landing now, an optional content module and SwiftUI components; NIP-25 reactions and media are not built. Platforms: Rust core is the truth; Swift qualified on macOS host (iOS-Simulator target pending); Kotlin is desktop-JVM only (no Android AAR yet).
HOW SOLID -- 100+ Rust test modules, differential falsifiers against an independent store, live-relay tests, cross-SDK parity, and a trusted-base surface-governance CI gate. Sharp edges: an M5 iOS main-thread jank fixed at the root but not re-measured on device; NIP-29 wins are microbenchmarks not device room-open; AUTH evidence states, cross-process reset exclusion, and full boundedness still open.
PERFORMANCE -- bounded memory and streaming, not first-N truncation. Busiest-room query 5.15ms to 0.26ms; rejected-heavy search 0.188ms to 0.005ms; derived-set resolver over 59,915 rows 3,786ms to 0.73ms. NIP-11 cache has a proven ~67 MiB raw-body ceiling. Public facade 29,734 lines against a 30,000 ceiling. CI green.
WHERE IT'S HEADING -- govern the provisional shapes toward a v2 freeze; encode lifecycle invariants as types; close platform qualification; standard secure-storage signer providers; finish bounded delivery.
THIS HOUR -- merged: #502 (typed FFI error taxonomy for NIP-11/NIP-46) and #503 (bool lifecycle gates re-encoded as Reservation/AsyncWait types). In flight, both green: #504 (SessionExecutor ownership type) and #488 (guarded NIP-51 favorite-relay editing).
item.itunes:title: NMP Briefing — 2026-07-15 11:30 UTC

item.title: NMP Pulse — 2026-07-15 11:00 UTC
item.description:
Progressing -- architecture-hardening sweep clears five merges this hour on clean CI, including one data-corruption-class fix that had merged uncaught before.
USER: NIP-02 follow/unfollow now behaves identically on iOS and Android -- apps built on nmp get consistent social-graph state cross-platform, today.
DEVELOPER: nmp is still two nouns -- live queries and write intents; describe intent, the engine owns relays, subscriptions, canonical state. Newly adoptable: RelayIngestResult now embeds the canonical CommittedMutationResult instead of hand-copying its fields, so relay-ingest and local-accept share one invalidation path.
THIS HOUR -- merged: #498 (arch-review guardrails + cross-SDK parity script), #499 (Kotlin NIP-02 parity), #500 (mutation-result unification), #501 (test-relay teardown state), #497 (live-store reset guard -- the corruption fix). In review: #502 (typed FFI error taxonomy), #503 (bool-lifecycle refactors) -- both green except swift-package still pending.
VERIFIED: the #490 epic's review named this violation class after one instance merged uncaught elsewhere; all five merges ride green CI (fmt, clippy, workspace tests) plus #498's new cross-language surface governance; #501 swaps a locale-fragile string match for real closed-state truth.
PERFORMANCE: build farm spiked to 14.78 one-minute load on 8 cores (likely this session's CI); 15-min average 4.79 is more typical. Public surface holds near 29.7k of the 30k-line ceiling.
NEXT HOUR: #502's typed FFI errors and #503's bool-lifecycle types are expected to land -- unblocking the queued #491 signer/FFI cleanup burst behind them.
item.itunes:title: NMP Pulse — 2026-07-15 11:00 UTC
